final_text:
Let me work through this step-by-step.

**Saturation magnetization** \(M_s = \frac{n \cdot \mu_B}{V}\) where \(n\) is the net magnetic moment per atom in Bohr magnetons.

Fe is BCC, so number of atoms per unit cell = 2.

Volume of unit cell = \(a^3 = (0.287 \times 10^{-9})^3 = 2.364 \times 10^{-29} \, \text{m}^3\)

Magnetic moment per unit cell = \(2 \times 2 \mu_B = 4 \mu_B\)

\(\mu_B = 9.273 \times 10^{-24} \, \text{A·m}^2\)

\(M_s = \frac{4 \times 9.273 \times 10^{-24}}{2.364 \times 10^{-29}} \, \text{A/m}\)

\(M_s = \frac{3.7092 \times 10^{-23}}{2.364 \times 10^{-29}} = 1.569 \times 10^{6} \, \text{A/m}\)

Convert to kA/m: \(1.569 \times 10^{6} \, \text{A/m} = 1569 \, \text{kA/m}\)

Rounded to one decimal place: 1569.0 kA/m

[ANSWER]1569.0[/ANSWER]
